title: A Woman's Hair. [1]
content:
Oh! little lock of golden hue
In gently waving ringlet curl'd,
By the dear head on which you grew,
I would not lose you for a world.
Not though a thousand more adorn
The polished brow where once you shone,
Like rays which guild a cloudless sky 
Beneath Columbia's fervid zone.
meaning:
This evocative piece by George Gordon Byron, titled "A Woman's Hair. [1]", represents a masterful exploration of classical-poetry. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
